children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary may establish a veterinary accreditation program that is consistent with this chapter, including the establishment of standards of conduct for accredited veterinarians.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: b Consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with State animal health officials and veterinary professionals regarding the establishment of the veterinary accreditation program.

children.heading: Suspension or revocation of accreditation
children.label: c Suspension or revocation of accreditation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may, after notice and opportunity for a hearing on the record, suspend or revoke the accreditation of any veterinarian accredited under this title who violates this chapter.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Final order
children.label: 2 Final order
children.text: The order of the Secretary suspending or revoking accreditation shall be treated as a final order reviewable under chapter 158 of title 28.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may summarily suspend the accreditation of a veterinarian whom the Secretary has reason to believe knowingly violated this chapter.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: B Hearings
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the veterinarian with a subsequent notice and an opportunity for a prompt post-suspension hearing on the record.

children.heading: Application of penalty provisions
children.label: d Application of penalty provisions
children.text: The criminal and civil penalties described in shall not apply to a violation of this section that is not a violation of any other provision of this chapter.section 8313 of this title
